objective: Remove TrueAlpha's reverse source dependency on infra2 and replace it with a versioned, side-effect-free deploy-request contract boundary.
claim_ceiling: E0 repository-boundary code and deterministic staging DeployRequest rendering only; no dispatch sender, receiver activation, deployment side effect, live-environment evidence, or Production-readiness claim

requirements: queued batch registration is merged to main before corpus preparation
the preparation PR freezes the exact repository-boundary corpus bytes
the implementation PR pins its exact current main base SHA
an independent reviewer is assigned before E0 acceptance
an active integration lease covers pyproject.toml and uv.lock
issue #201 contains this manifest path and exact SHA-256
Delivery Governance CI passes offline validation and live GitHub parity

later_claim_dependencies: infra2 must explicitly enable and verify TrueAlpha requests before any repository_dispatch sender is activated
issue #11 still owns isolated Staging runtime and operator evidence
issues #52, #67, and #68 still own recovery, Production shadow, and independent capture evidence

required_strata: SDK v0.1.0 immutable release pin and installed distribution identity
valid TrueAlpha staging DeployRequest v1 round trip
invalid service, repository, ref, SHA, and evidence rejection
production request rejection before remote evidence verification and receiver enablement
absence of the repo gitlink, .gitmodules entry, and source-execution documentation

source_ceiling: Checked-in contract fixtures and local Git metadata only; no repository dispatch, image publication, infrastructure mutation, or live environment access.

release_activation.reason: This batch changes repository and contract tooling only; no accepted release binding, sender workflow, runtime service, schedule, or environment activation may reference it.

negative_controls: repo remains a gitlink or .gitmodules still names infra2
infra2 source is imported, checked out, or executed by TrueAlpha tooling
SDK dependency is mutable or lacks a lockfile hash
malformed service, source repository, ref, SHA, or evidence is serialized
production or live dispatch can be enabled by a CLI argument
deployment, image publication, runtime activation, or readiness promotion occurs

invalidation: infra2-sdk version, hash, DeployRequest schema, source repository, or service identity change creates a new batch revision
receiver authority or production evidence policy change reruns the exact negative controls
any runtime, image, workflow, environment, or release activation expands scope and requires a separately authorized batch
rollback: Revert the repository-boundary implementation and restore the prior gitlink only if remote infra invocation cannot be maintained; no runtime data, image, or infrastructure state is mutated by this batch.
